title: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same
display_title: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same
wikitext:
[]: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by OneShotPunch » Sun Aug 11, 2024 12:08 am If we are to treat the TV ending and EoE as seperate endings (as Thrice Upon a Time's title implies), then what happened in the TV version of events that lead to Instrumentality? What's the divergence point? What happens after Shinji accepts he wants to continue existing in this world? Yes, there is evidence they might very well be concurrent, but that isn't the question being asked.

[]: Re: If EoE TV and EoE movie are not the same...             Quote Post by Superblunt » Sun Aug 11, 2024 8:18 am You might wanna change the title, you are talking about the original Episodes 25 and 26. THere IS an End of Evangelion TV version, it's on one of the original Japanese DVD releases, where the movie is edited into TV episodes like the show.

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by OneShotPunch » Mon Aug 12, 2024 10:41 am You might wanna change the title, you are talking about the original Episodes 25 and 26. THere IS an End of Evangelion TV version, it's on one of the original Japanese DVD releases, where the movie is edited into TV episodes like the show.

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Aug 12, 2024 5:28 pm What's the divergence point?

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by OneShotPunch » Thu Aug 22, 2024 10:47 am He's quite passive here, like he just chose not to do anything and to stay curled into a ball & to ignore everything, and because of that "no one was saved".

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by Tim Julius » Thu Aug 29, 2024 2:43 am OneShotPunch wrote: If we are to treat the TV ending and EoE as seperate endings (as Thrice Upon a Time's title implies), then what happened in the TV version of events that lead to Instrumentality? What's the divergence point? What happens after Shinji accepts he wants to continue existing in this world? Yes, there is evidence they might very well be concurrent, but that isn't the question being asked.

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Aug 29, 2024 8:55 am Tim Julius wrote: The divergence lies in its focus on psychological rebirth, while EoE emphasizes external consequences.

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Tue Dec 31, 2024 11:07 pm OneShotPunch wrote: If we are to treat the TV ending and EoE as seperate endings (as Thrice Upon a Time's title implies), then what happened in the TV version of events that lead to Instrumentality? What's the divergence point? What happens after Shinji accepts he wants to continue existing in this world? Yes, there is evidence they might very well be concurrent, but that isn't the question being asked.

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by viperzero » Tue Dec 31, 2024 11:19 pm To tut my own horn I wrote a long article why both endings are the same. They both build on psychological concepts introduced from episode 16 on. I think the focus is different with the TV being more direct and explanatory and EoE being the more raw and emotional. thread/21760/Why-both-endings-are-the-same-and-the-psychology-behind-them/

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Feb 11, 2025 11:13 pm The diversion potentially goes back as far as episode 21, as the Director's Cut versions of 21-24 added additional scenes that retcon how Instrumentality is portrayed to better match EoE's storyline. Also Lilith grows legs when the Spear is removed, for some reason. According to an old interview, the original plan for the ending was for the events of EoE up to the halfway point credits to be episode 25, and for the televised episode 26 to be exactly as it was. Budget and network restrictions led to the televised episode 25. This is why there are references to EoE's story in episode 25, but nothing from the second half or in 26. The suppression of the OA cuts of 21-24 in the recent international releases really do a disservice to 25 and 26, since a completely different version of Instrumentality is set up instead.

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by 2Lacissal2 » Wed Feb 26, 2025 12:19 pm In an interview with Megumi Hayashibara after the TV broadcast ended Anno was fairly explicit on EoTV and what would become EoE being divergent, different endings, like a multi-ending game or game with branching story paths. Source thread: https://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10510/Anno-EoE-gives-the-same-end-as-the-TV-series/40/ ] SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same...             Quote Post by viperzero » Wed Feb 26, 2025 3:51 pm I agree that both endings are not literally two perspectives on one event. EoE has a markedly darker tone and Shinji is obviously in a worse off mental state. But I do think thematically they are the same. Shinji learns his perspective on himself in the world is variable and he doesn’t have to view either so negatively. He learns the possibility of loving himself.

Article: If the TV ending and EoE are not the same
11:13 pm The diversion potentially goes back as far as episode 21, as the Director's Cut versions of 21-24 added additional scenes that retcon how Instrumentality is portrayed to better match EoE's storyline. Also Lilith grows legs when the Spear is removed, for some reason. According to an old interview, the original plan for the ending was for the events of EoE up to the halfway point credits to be episode 25, and for the televised episode 26 to be exactly as it was. Budget and network restrictions led to the televised episode 25. This is why there are references to EoE's story in episode 25, but nothing from the second half or in 26. The suppression of the OA cuts of 21-24 in the recent international releases really do a disservice to 25 and 26, since a completely different version of Instrumentality is set up instead.
